title: THE SMALLEST QUANTUM MACKEY DEFORMATION YVANNGAUDILLOT–ESTRADA Abstract. Onthebasisofideas of Mackey [25], Higson exhibited striking similarities between the representation theories of G and G in the complex case [19]. This analogy, named after Mackey, 0 was later carried out by Afgoustidis for real groups [1–3]. One of its main features is the existence of a natural bijection mapping continuously (see [4]) the tempered (or reduced unitary) dual of G to the unitary dual of G which preserves minimal 0 K-types. The Mackey analogy also led to a new proof of the Connes-Kasparov isomorphism: the contraction of G to G gives rise to a continuous field of reduced 0 group C*-algebras, called the Mackey deformation, which has constant K-theory.
